headline: Anaconda Copper Mining Company Shuts Down, 1903
body:
The air in Butte, Montana, on October 23, 1903, hung heavy not with the usual acrid scent of copper smoke, but with the chilling silence of impending destitution. Fifteen thousand men, their hands calloused from years of wresting wealth from the earth, found themselves abruptly cast into the unforgiving maw of unemployment. The Anaconda Copper Mining Company, that monolithic entity which had long held the state in its grasp, had flexed its immense power, shutting down every mine, smelter, and operation across Montana. It was a calculated, brutal maneuver, a direct consequence of the ongoing War of the Copper Kings, a battle for control waged between the Amalgamated Copper Company (the Anaconda's parent, backed by Standard Oil) and the defiant F. Augustus Heinze.

Heinze, a shrewd and audacious mining magnate, had dared to challenge the Amalgamated's dominion, leveraging the intricate
